title: ajuga chamaepitys subsp. suffrutescens
intro: Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. suffrutescens, also known as bugleweed or small bugle, is a perennial plant belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The health benefits of Ajuga chamaepitys subsp. suffrutescens include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve heart health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever and respiratory issues.
